body:
## Summary

Fixes #1092

When a user types `-- model opus` (with a space after `--`) instead of `--model opus`, the system now detects this malformed pattern and produces a helpful error message with a suggestion, instead of silently using the default model.

### The Problem

In the reported issue, a user typed `/solve https://... -- model opus` and the system:
1. Tokenized `-- model` into `['--', 'model']` (split by space)
2. Yargs interpreted `--` as the POSIX "end of options" marker (valid)
3. `model` became a positional argument (silently ignored)
4. The system used the default model (Sonnet 4.5) instead of Opus 4.5

### The Solution

Added `detectMalformedFlags()` function that catches:
- `"-- option"` as a single argument (space after `--`)
- `['--', 'option']` as split tokens (Issue #1092 exact scenario)
- `"-option"` (single dash for long option)
- `"---option"` (triple dash)
- `"- -option"` (space between dashes)

The function provides helpful error messages like:
```
Malformed option "-- model": Space after "--". Did you mean "--model"?
```

### Changes

1. **src/option-suggestions.lib.mjs** - Added `detectMalformedFlags()` function and `KNOWN_OPTION_NAMES` list
2. **src/solve.config.lib.mjs** - Integrated malformed flag detection into CLI argument parsing
3. **src/telegram-bot.mjs** - Integrated malformed flag detection into Telegram bot
4. **tests/test-malformed-flags.mjs** - Added 23 unit tests covering various scenarios
5. **experiments/test-malformed-args.mjs** - Experiment script used during development
6. **docs/case-studies/issue-1092/** - Case study documentation with analysis

### Test Plan

- [x] All 23 new unit tests pass
- [x] Existing solve tests still pass
- [x] ESLint passes
- [x] Prettier formatting passes
- [x] Edge cases tested (valid POSIX `--` marker, non-option words after `--`, etc.)

### Test Commands

```bash
# Run new malformed flag tests
node tests/test-malformed-flags.mjs

# Run existing solve tests
node tests/test-solve.mjs
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Adding CLAUDE.md with task information for AI processing.
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1092

When a user types "-- model" (with a space after --) instead of "--model",
the system now detects this malformed pattern and produces a helpful error
message with a suggestion, instead of silently using the default model.

Changes:
- Added detectMalformedFlags() function in option-suggestions.lib.mjs
- Integrated detection into both Telegram bot and CLI argument parsing
- Added comprehensive unit tests (23 tests)
- Created case study documentation in docs/case-studies/issue-1092/

The fix catches:
- "-- option" as single arg (space after --)
- ["--", "option"] as split tokens (POSIX-style, Issue #1092 exact scenario)
- "-option" (single dash for long option)
- "---option" (triple dash)
- "- -option" (space between dashes)

Fixes #1092
